perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall be jointly owned by the Disclosing Party and Receiving Party during the term of exploring the business relationship.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The modified text introduces a conflicting concept of joint ownership. This now contradicts section 5 which assumes the Disclosing party has the right to ask for the documents back.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information delivered to and/or in the possession of the Receiving Party shall be returned or delivered to the Disclosing Party, with all copies made thereof, in whatever form, if the Disclosing Party so requests.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. The changed text introduces joint ownership of the Confidential Information by both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that all Confidential Information in the possession of the Receiving Party shall be returned to the Disclosing Party if requested. Joint ownership implies rights to the information that conflict with the Disclosing Party's right to demand its return.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall: (a) hold the Confidential Information in confidence and not disclose it to third parties, except in the limited cases referred to in paragraph "6"; and (b) not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than exploring or examining the possibility of a business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall: (a) make reasonable efforts to hold the Confidential Information in confidence and may disclose it to third parties, provided they are informed of the confidential nature of the information; and (b) may use the Confidential Information for internal business purposes in addition to exploring the business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure to third parties and limits the use of information to exploring a business relationship. The modified text softens the confidentiality obligation ('reasonable efforts') and expands permitted use to internal business purposes and introduces the permissibility of disclosure to third parties. This contradiction makes the definition of breach of the contract confusing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that the Confidential Information shall be disclosed to only those people within its respective organizations or its agents, consultants, representatives or advisors who have a need to know the information 2 <PAGE> and who are obligated under terms no less restrictive than those imposed by this Agreement on the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure to third parties. The changed text allows disclosure to third parties if they are informed of the confidential nature of the information. The contradicted text states that Confidential Information shall be disclosed to only those people within its respective organizations or its agents who have a need to know the information. Since the changed text broadens the scope of permissible disclosure beyond those with a 'need to know' within the organizations or its agents, this directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text's prohibition aligns with the 'need to know' limitation, but the change introduces a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Either party hereto shall have the right, at any time, to terminate in writing and discussions and exchange of information in connection with the exploration of the possibilities of a business relationship between the parties without any further obligations or liabilities to the other party, other than the obligations of confidentiality hereunder, or any right or obligation relating to the Confidential Information hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party hereto shall have the right, at any time, to terminate discussions and exchange of information in connection with the exploration of the possibilities of a business relationship between the parties. However, any termination requires a 30-day written notice and may be subject to termination fees as outlined in Appendix B.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows termination at any time without further obligation (except for confidentiality). The modified text introduces a 30-day written notice requirement and potential termination fees. This directly contradicts the original 'without any further obligations or liabilities,' creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party hereto shall have the right, at any time, to terminate in writing and discussions and exchange of information in connection with the exploration of the possibilities of a business relationship between the parties without any further obligations or liabilities to the other party, other than the obligations of confidentiality hereunder, or any right or obligation relating to the Confidential Information hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that termination could occur at any time without further obligation. The changed text introduces a 30-day written notice requirement and potential termination fees (Appendix B), which directly contradict the 'without any further obligations or liabilities' clause that is found in the contradicted text. Therefore, according to the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction,' a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Paragraph "4" hereof, the obligation of the Receiving Party provided in Paragraph "2" hereof shall continue for three (3) years from the date of each receipt of the Confidential Information, even after termination of this Agreement according to paragraph "3" hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of the Receiving Party provided in Paragraph "2" hereof shall continue indefinitely from the date of each receipt of the Confidential Information, even after termination of this Agreement according to paragraph "3" hereof, unless explicitly waived in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits the confidentiality obligation to three years. The modified version extends this obligation indefinitely, unless waived. This contradicts the fixed three-year term specified in the original text, creating an in-text contradiction in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of the above paragraph "2" shall not apply to any information which: (a) is available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party; or (b) was in the possession of the Receiving Party prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party; or (c) is received independently from a third party who is free to disclose such information to the Receiving Party; or (d) is subsequently independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) has been or is made public by the Disclosing Party, such as by commercial use or sale or by publications or patents, or otherwise; or (f) is approved for release by written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the confidentiality obligation to three years. The changed text extends this obligation indefinitely, unless waived. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which lists specific conditions under which the confidentiality obligations in paragraph "2" do not apply. The indefinite obligation now contradicts the exceptions because the receiving party is now liable for an indefinite amount of time, even if exceptions (a)-(f) apply to the information.
